comment_text.sn38.2:1.2: Arahatta, the state of being an arahant.

root_text.sn38.2:1.5: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā etassa arahattassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.2:1.6: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā etassa arahattassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.2:1.7: “Katamo panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā etassa arahattassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.2:1.8: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo etassa arahattassa sacchikiriyāya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.2:1.10: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo, ayaṁ paṭipadā etassa arahattassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.2:1.11: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā etassa arahattassa sacchikiriyāya.

translation_text.sn38.2:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘perfection’.
translation_text.sn38.2:1.3: “Reverend, the ending of greed, hate, and delusion
translation_text.sn38.2:1.5: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for realizing this perfection?”
translation_text.sn38.2:1.8: “It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn38.2:1.9: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn38.2:1.10: This is the path, the practice, for realizing this perfection.”
translation_text.sn38.2:1.11: “Reverend, this is a fine path, a fine practice, for realizing this perfection.
translation_text.sn38.2:1.12: Just this much is enough for diligence.”
